title: Needle Exchange and Injection Supplies - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Needle Exchange and Injection Supplies 29 Matching Service Providers BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides syringe exchange services, injectable naloxone and fentanyl test strips, and peer support for substance abuse. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 8am-8pm, Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-5pm, Wednesday 8am-7pm; closed on holidays Email (541) 766-6314 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER MEDICAL SERVICES AND NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mobile medical care and needle exchange by hosting Central City Concern's and Outside In's medical vans and syringe exchange program. Includes a full range of health services such as disease management, routine primary care, HIV/STI testing, women's health care, mental health, and transgender care. Also provides assistance with enrollment in Oregon Health Plan (OHP) or other health insurance plans. For the syringe exchange, users get an even exchange for however many syringes they bring in, though no one will be turned away for not having returns. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: -Medical van: Age 13 and older. Low-income individuals and households. Must not be receiving primary care from another provider. -Needle exchange: Ages 18 and older Hours: Medical van: Tuesday 9am-3pm Needle exchange: Tuesday/Thursday 1pm-4pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CLATSOP COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH AND MOBILE UNIT HARM REDUCTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Conducts a syringe exchange in which participants are provided clean syringes on a one-for one basis (one clean syringe for one used syringe). Naloxone training and kits and other recource referrals are also available at all locations. 820 Exchange Street Suite 100 Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Astoria: Thursday 2pm-3:30pmpm-3:30pm Seaside: Thursday 11:30am-1pm Email (503) 325-8500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CLATSOP COUNTY PUBLIC SOUTH COUNTY CENTER HARM REDUCTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Conducts a syringe exchange in which participants are provided clean syringes on a one-for one basis (one clean syringe for one used syringe). Naloxone training and kits and other recource referrals are also available at all locations. 1225 Avenue A Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Clatsop County Public Health Astoria: Thursday 2pm-3:30pm Seaside: Thursday 11:30am-1pm Email (503) 325-8500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY OUTREACH THROUGH RADICAL EMPOWERMENT STREET OUTREACH ALLIANCE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management/coordinated care, community trainings/education, Naloxone distribution/overdose education, safe sex items, syringe services, and harm reduction supplies. Eligibility: Ages 16-29 experiencing homelessness or housing instability Hours: Requests for supplies: Tuesday 10am-2pm Supply drops occur on Tuesday 4pm-7pm Email (541) 870-0036 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY OUTREACH THROUGH RADICAL EMPOWERMENT HARM REDUCTION DISTRIBUTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Distributes harm reduction supplies and offers education about safer use. Provides condoms, clean needles, Narcan/Naloxone, and assists with resource navigation. Also provides supplies that are specific to individuals in the life/sex work. Eligibility: Ages 16-29 Hours: No standard hours, by appointment only Email (541) 870-0036 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The harm reduction program is anonymous, confidential and designed to reduce the risks involved in drug use. Services include safe injection and smoking supplies, including sterile syringes, sharps containers, and many other items. The program also provides access to naloxone, fentanyl test strips and overdose prevention education; connections to same-time medical care, HIV and Hepatitis C testing, vaccines, education, and referral to community resources and services. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: People who use drugs. Must be at least 18 years of age. Hours: Tuesdays, La Pine at 51425 Highway 97, 10am-12pm; Wednesdays, Downtown Bend at 680 NW Bond Street, 9am-11am; Thursdays, Downtown Redmond at 406 West Antler Avenue, 2pm-4pm Email (541) 322-7400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH DOWNTOWN HEALTH CENTER REDMOND HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The harm reduction program is anonymous, confidential and designed to reduce the risks involved in drug use. Services include safe injection and smoking supplies, including sterile syringes, sharps containers, and many other items. The program also provides access to naloxone, fentanyl test strips and overdose prevention education; connections to same-time medical care, HIV and Hepatitis C testing, vaccines, education, and referral to community resources and services. 236 NW Kingwood Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: People who use drugs. Must be at least 18 years of age. Hours: Tuesdays, La Pine at 51425 Highway 97, 10am-12pm; Wednesdays, Downtown Bend at 680 NW Bond Street, 9am-11am; Thursdays, Downtown Redmond at 406 West Antler Avenue, 2pm-4pm Email (541) 322-7400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supplies and education for safer drug use, including syringe exchange, disposal, and testing for HIV. Offers referrals for additional services, including detox and recovery programs. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING LA GRANDE NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supplies and education for safer drug use, including syringe exchange, disposal, and testing for HIV. Offers referrals for additional services, including detox and recovery programs. 2102 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 612-4028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING ONTARIO NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supplies and education for safer drug use, including syringe exchange, disposal, and testing for HIV. Offers referrals for additional services, including detox and recovery programs. 1021 SW 5th Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING PENDLETON NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supplies and education for safer drug use, including syringe exchange, disposal, and testing for HIV. Offers referrals for additional services, including detox and recovery programs. 322 SW 3rd Avenue Pendleton, OR 978012174 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING THE DALLES NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supplies and education for safer drug use, including syringe exchange, disposal, and testing for HIV. Offers referrals for additional services, including detox and recovery programs. 400 E Scenic Drive The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY ASSISTANCE AND RESOURCE CENTER GROUP NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers outreach and harm-reduction services including a mobile needle exchange, education on safer drug use, testing services, and connections to detox and recovery programs. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm Email (541) 224-5538 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIV ALLIANCE HIV ALLIANCE DOUGLAS COUNTY NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential needle exchange program including disposal of used syringes and distribution of new syringes and equipment (sharps containers, alcohol preps, and first aid supplies). Also provides drug checking supplies, naloxone and overdose response training, HIV, hepatitis C, and syphilis testing, and safer sex supplies. 647 W Luellen Drive Suite 3 Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who use drugs or inject substances such as hormones or steroids, and their friends and family members Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 342-5088 162 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIV ALLIANCE HIV ALLIANCE JOSEPHINE COUNTY CAVE JUNCTION HARM REDUCTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers harm reduction supplies such as sharps containers, drug test strips, and safe use supplies. Safe use supplies may include tourniquets and cookers. Other supplies offered include hygiene kits, wound care kits, safe smoking kits, and condoms with and without latex. Availability of all items may vary based on supply stock. 200 W Watkins Street Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-7pm Temporary location: Tuesday 3pm-7pm at Great Circle Recovery 1011 Commercial Street NE, Suite 110, Salem, OR 97301 (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIV ALLIANCE HIV ALLIANCE JOSEPHINE COUNTY GRANTS PASS NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential needle exchange program including disposal of used syringes and distribution of new syringes and equipment (sharps containers, alcohol preps, and first aid supplies). Also provides drug checking supplies, naloxone and overdose response training, HIV, hepatitis C, and syphilis testing, and safer sex supplies. 132 NE B Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who use drugs or inject substances such as hormones or steroids, and their friends and family members Hours: Monday-Friday 1pm-4pm (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIV ALLIANCE HIV ALLIANCE LANE COUNTY NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential needle exchange program including disposal of used syringes and distribution of new syringes and equipment (sharps containers, alcohol preps, and first aid supplies). Also provides drug checking supplies, naloxone and overdose response training, HIV, hepatitis C, and syphilis testing, and safer sex supplies. 1195 City View Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who use drugs or inject substances such as hormones or steroids, and their friends and family members Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Friday 10am-2pm Email (541) 342-5088 209 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIV ALLIANCE HIV ALLIANCE MARION COUNTY NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential needle exchange program including disposal of used syringes and distribution of new syringes and equipment (sharps containers, alcohol preps, and first aid supplies). Also provides drug checking supplies, naloxone and overdose response training, HIV, hepatitis C, and syphilis testing, and safer sex supplies. 3855 Wolverine Street NE Suite 6 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who use drugs or inject substances such as hormones or steroids, and their friends and family members Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-7pm Additional temporary services: Tuesday 3pm-7pm, Friday 10am-noon at Great Circle Recovery 1011 Commercial Street NE, Suite 110, Salem, OR 97301 Email (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIV ALLIANCE HIV ALLIANCE MARION COUNTY HARM REDUCTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers harm reduction supplies such as sharps containers, drug test strips, and safe use supplies. Safe use supplies may include tourniquets and cookers. Other supplies offered include hygiene kits, wound care kits, safe smoking kits, and condoms with and without latex. Availability of all items may vary based on supply stock. 3855 Wolverine Street NE Suite 6 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-7pm Temporary location: Tuesday 3pm-7pm at Great Circle Recovery 1011 Commercial Street NE, Suite 110, Salem, OR 97301 Email (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH SYRINGE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides safe disposal of used syringes, access to sterile syringes, and new safer injection supplies and sharps containers. Naloxone and other overdose rescue kits are available. Services are availabe on-site and also at various pop-up clinics. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: On-site: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 2pm-4pm Pop-up clinics: Varies Email (541) 774-3333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH SYRINGE EXCHANGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services include used points exchange for new points, wound care supplies, safer sex supplies, risk reduction counseling, and referrals to medical care services, such as sexually transmitted disease screening, HIV testing, mental health services, alcohol and drug treatment, medication assisted treatment, and naloxone. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Intravenous drug users age 18 and older, none for HIV testing Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 2pm-4pm HIV testing by appointment: Monday-Friday 9-11am, 1pm-4pm Email (541) 774-8209 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINCOLN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV/HEPATITIS C TESTING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers testing for HIV and Hepatitis C, as well as a syringe exchange services. Site offers distribution of condoms and first aid supplies. Female condoms available upon request. 255 SW Coast Highway Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies; By appointment only Email (541) 265-0404 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK HARM REDUCTION/INJECTION SUPPLY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an injection and harm reduction supply and exchange program. Provides new needles/syringes for hormone replacement therapy and sharp containers and accepts full sharp containers for needle/syringe disposal. Also offers other safety and safe sex items like Narcan kits, fentanyl testing strips, band-aids, alcohol swabs, condoms (latex and non-latex), dental dams, lube, and HIV at-home test kits. 260 SW Madison Avenue Suite 110 Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as LGBTQ+ or gender diverse Hours: Sunday-Friday noon-5pm or by appointment Email (541) 264-1973 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT MULTNOMAH COUNTY HARM REDUCTION CLINIC HARM REDUCTION CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term care for individuals with skin, wound, and soft tissue infections. Provides HIV, hepatitis C, and syphilis testing. Other services include syringe exchange, Naloxone distribution, health insurance enrollment, and referral to community resources. 12425 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Primary focus is injection drug users, but anyone can request services Hours: Monday/Thursday 11am-5pm (503) 988-0577 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Syringe exchange, naloxone and fentanyl test strips distribution. 619 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Thursday 11am-2pm, and 3pm-7pm Email (503) 988-7099 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN INJECTION DRUG USERS HEALTH SERVICES NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a syringe exchange to prevent the spread of HIV and other diseases among drug injectors. In addition to safer injection supplies, participants can receive counseling, safer sex information and supplies, free and anonymous HIV testing and Hepatitis C testing, as well as other drug treatment referrals. Outside In's syringe exchange policy is a one for one (1:1) program, meaning users get an even exchange for the amount of syringes they bring in, however no one will be turned away for not having returns. 1219 SW Main Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (503) 535-3826 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN INJECTION DRUG USERS HEALTH SERVICES NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a syringe exchange to prevent the spread of HIV and other diseases among drug injectors. In addition to safer injection supplies, participants can receive counseling, safer sex information and supplies, free and anonymous HIV testing and Hepatitis C testing, as well as other drug treatment referrals. Outside In's syringe exchange policy is a one for one (1:1) program, meaning users get an even exchange for the amount of syringes they bring in, however no one will be turned away for not having returns. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (503) 535-3826 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN INJECTION DRUG USERS HEALTH SERVICES NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a syringe exchange to prevent the spread of HIV and other diseases among drug injectors. In addition to safer injection supplies, participants can receive counseling, safer sex information and supplies, free and anonymous HIV testing and Hepatitis C testing, as well as other drug treatment referrals. Outside In's syringe exchange policy is a one for one (1:1) program, meaning users get an even exchange for the amount of syringes they bring in, however no one will be turned away for not having returns. 700 Molalla Avenue Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday 11am-2pm Email (503) 535-3826 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND PEOPLES OUTREACH PROJECT HARM REDUCTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sterile syringes, Naloxone (Narcan), and other harm-reduction supplies directly to users through volunteers doing outreach on foot and bicycles. Unlike many needle exchanges, this is need-based, meaning users do not have to dispose of dirty syringes to receive new ones. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Friday Bicycle/Van Outreach: 6pm-7pm Eastbank Esplanade at SE Main Street 7:25pm-7:50pm Greyhound/Bud Clark Center at NW 6th and Hoyt Streets 8pm-8:45pm West side; text or call for location Saturday St. Johns: 1pm-4pm The Cut (Peninsula Crossing Trail), on the bike path just west of N. Lombard Street and Macrum Avenue; location sometimes changes, call to verify Sunday Driving and Foot Outreach: 2:30pm-3:15pm Hollywood Transit Center end of NE Weidler off 42nd Avenue 3:45pm-4:30pm South Park Blocks at SW Park and Jefferson 4:45pm-5:30pm Greyhound/Bud Clark at NW 6th and Hoyt Streets Email (503) 765-7767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK COUNTY COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTERS PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION AND SYRINGE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides new needles and a safe sharps disposal service. Offers hygiene and harm reduction supplies, including alcohol swabs, condoms, wound care items, and Naloxone/Narcan training and kits. Connects individuals with community resources and recovery support. All services are anonymous. 2111 8th Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Tillamook 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month 11am-1pm The Herald Center 2710 First Street Tillamook, OR 97141, -Pacific City 1st Thursday of the month 11am-1pm Brooten Road Pacific City, OR 97135 -Wheeler 3rd Thursday of the month 11am-1pm Parking lot above North County Food Bank 278 Rowe St, Wheeler, OR 97147 Email (503) 842-3943 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER MEDICAL SERVICES AND NEEDLE EXCHANGE CLATSOP COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION CLATSOP COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING NEEDLE EXCHANGE EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING NEEDLE EXCHANGE EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING NEEDLE EXCHANGE EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING NEEDLE EXCHANGE EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING NEEDLE EXCHANGE HIV ALLIANCE NEEDLE EXCHANGE HIV ALLIANCE HARM REDUCTION HIV ALLIANCE NEEDLE EXCHANGE HIV ALLIANCE NEEDLE EXCHANGE HIV ALLIANCE NEEDLE EXCHANGE JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH SYRINGE EXCHANGE JACKSON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH SYRINGE EXCHANGE PROGRAM LINCOLN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV/HEPATITIS C TESTING MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK HARM REDUCTION/INJECTION SUPPLY PROGRAM MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HARM REDUCTION CLINIC MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT NEEDLE EXCHANGE OUTSIDE IN NEEDLE EXCHANGE OUTSIDE IN NEEDLE EXCHANGE OUTSIDE IN NEEDLE EXCHANGE TILLAMOOK COUNTY COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTERS PUBLIC HEALTH HARM REDUCTION AND SYRINGE SERVICES
